claim: smolagents framework supports multiple LLM providers (Claude, Gemini, OpenAI, Ollama/local); small models struggle with code agents, work better with tool-calling agents
actionable: When building with smolagents, use tool-calling agents for small models (Llama 3.2 3B); use code agents only with Claude Sonnet or GPT-4-tier models
source_title: How to make Muilt-Agent Apps with smolagents

claim: Tool definitions must be precise: require type hints, use 'args' not 'parameters', describe each argument exactly; fussiness in decorator config prevents runtime failures
actionable: Create tools with strict type annotations and clear argument descriptions; test tool schemas before passing to multi-agent orchestrator
source_title: How to make Muilt-Agent Apps with smolagents

claim: Manager agent (code agent) directs multiple managed agents (tool-calling agents); manager knows entire execution graph, agents are dumb and just execute tasks
actionable: Structure multi-agent systems with a smart manager (code agent) orchestrating dumb specialists (tool-calling agents with few tools each)
source_title: How to make Muilt-Agent Apps with smolagents

claim: Small models (Qwen 2.5 Coda 7B) churn through tokens doing multiple observation loops, often stumble around; GPT-4 mini is best cost/performance for multi-agent
actionable: For cost-effective multi-agent, use GPT-4o mini; avoid small local models for anything beyond simple tool calling
source_title: How to make Muilt-Agent Apps with smolagents

claim: Multi-hop reasoning (e.g., 'find movie from 1955, then its Rotten Tomatoes score') works well with multi-agent + proper manager agent that coordinates across hops
actionable: Break multi-step queries into explicit agent tasks; have manager orchestrate each step and pass results between agents
source_title: How to make Muilt-Agent Apps with smolagents

claim: Gradio UI integration lets you build web interfaces for agents without front-end code; agents can use text-to-image and other visual tools
actionable: Use Gradio to expose agent as web app; declare tools like text-to-image, and agents can invoke them within Gradio chat interface
source_title: How to make Muilt-Agent Apps with smolagents

claim: Blog-writer multi-agent example: research agent → research-checker agent → writer agent → copy-editor agent; each agent has specific role, manager coordinates all
actionable: Design specialized agents for each stage of complex task (research, validate, write, edit); manager ensures proper sequencing and handoff
source_title: How to make Muilt-Agent Apps with smolagents

claim: Custom tools via Gina AI scraping service: can swap out tool providers easily by respecting the tool interface, enabling flexible multi-agent composition
actionable: Build agent systems with pluggable tool interfaces so you can swap implementations (e.g., DuckDuckGo vs Gina scraping) without rewriting agents
source_title: How to make Muilt-Agent Apps with smolagents
